title: P69-10 fix: in hosteddashboard, delete the array element ...
review_summary: Reviewer approved the plan for Tomonkyo and execution can proceed.
request_text: P69-10 fix: in hosteddashboard, delete the array element with id "master1" from src/data/mockUsers.js, and in src/pages/Dashboard.js move the top-level localStorage.getItem currentUser read into a useEffect inside the Dashboard component. Touch only those two files. Do not create new files. Apply the patch.

translation_json.normalized_request: P69-10: In hosteddashboard, delete array element with id 'master1' from src/data/mockUsers.js, and move top-level localStorage.getItem currentUser read into a useEffect inside src/pages/Dashboard.js component. Apply patch to only those two files.
translation_json.intent: Apply targeted code changes to remove hardcoded user 'master1' and fix localStorage timing in Dashboard component
translation_json.objective: Remove hardcoded 'master1' user from mockUsers.js and move localStorage read into useEffect for proper React lifecycle handling
constraints: Delete array element with id 'master1' from mockUsers.js
Move localStorage.getItem currentUser read from top-level to useEffect inside Dashboard component
requested_outputs: Modified src/data/mockUsers.js with master1 element removed
Modified src/pages/Dashboard.js with localStorage read in useEffect

plan_json.objective: Implement the Jira issue by generating code changes, applying patches, running tests, and reviewing results.
plan_json.request_summary: P69-10 fix: in hosteddashboard, delete the array element with id "master1" from src/data/mockUsers.js, and in src/pag...
plan_json.change_summary: Remove hardcoded master1 user from mockUsers.js and refactor Dashboard.js to read localStorage inside useEffect for proper React lifecycle handling
plan_json.change_explanation: This fix addresses P69-10 by eliminating the hardcoded 'master1' administrative user from mockUsers.js and correcting the localStorage timing issue in Dashboard.js by moving the read into a useEffect hook, preventing it from executing during server-side rendering or before the component mounts.
assumptions: The referenced Jira issue exists and contains enough context to generate code.
Affected source files can be read from the knowledge index or sandbox.

tools.purpose: Generate code changes that implement the Jira issue requirements.

steps.title: Analyze Jira issue and identify affected files
steps.expected_output: Identified locations of master1 element in mockUsers.js and localStorage.getItem call in Dashboard.js with specific line references
steps.success_criteria: Both target files and modification locations are confirmed with exact line numbers

steps.title: Generate code patch for both files
steps.expected_output: Patch generated removing master1 object from mockUsers.js array and relocating localStorage.getItem into useEffect in Dashboard.js
steps.success_criteria: Patch contains valid edits for both target files without modifying any other files

steps.title: Apply patch and run tests in sandbox
steps.expected_output: Patch applied successfully to both files; sandbox tests pass confirming no regressions
steps.success_criteria: Both files modified correctly; test suite reports no failures related to these changes

steps.title: Review generated changes
steps.expected_output: Verified master1 removed from mockUsers.js and localStorage read properly moved into useEffect in Dashboard.js
steps.success_criteria: Code review confirms changes are correct, complete, and only touch the two specified files

review_json.summary: Reviewer approved the plan for Tomonkyo and execution can proceed.

policy_checks.detail: codegen.generate_patch is registered under codegen with write access.
